data.title: Dollar General declares $0.59 dividend
data.description:
• Dollar General (DG) declared $0.59/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.15%
  • Payable Jan. 20; for shareholders of record Jan. 6; ex-div Jan. 6.
  • See DG Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Salesforce rises after 'solid' Q3 keeps analysts bullish
data.description:
Shares of Salesforce (CRM) rose about 2% premarket on Thursday after the company's results and outlook drew positive views from analysts.

Wedbush kept its Outperform rating and $375 price target on the shares of the software giant.

"CRM reported its FY3Q26 results with the top-line coming in line with Street expectations while seeing a significant bottom-line beat with better-than-expected FY4Q26 top-line guidance as the company continues to invest aggressively in its Agentforce strategy to reaccelerate growth," said analysts led by Dan Ives.

The analysts added that Salesforce provided fourth quarter fiscal 2026 guidance which came in above Street estimates on the top-line while meeting the Street’s EPS estimate as the company balances investments into growth with margin expansion and free cash flow, or FCF, generation.

"This was a step in the right direction for CRM with a long way to go as the Agentforce strategy builds incremental momentum with a strong pipeline to capitalize on while balancing investments into growth initiatives with bottom-line expansion over the coming quarters," said Ives and his team.

Evercore maintained its Outperform rating but lowered the price target on the stock to $340 from $360.

Analysts led by Kirk Materne said Salesforce delivered "solid" fiscal third quarter results, adding that current remaining performance obligations, or cRPO, growth of 11%/11% in constant currency, or cc, versus Evercore/Street estimates of ~10.2% or 9% in cc was a solid nearly 200 basis points, or bps, beat in cc aided modestly by early renewals, but the bulk was driven by strong bookings.

"Clearly, there is risk that CRM shares remain stuck in the mud, but we see the upside materially outweighing the downside. Following a solid F3Q and building Agentforce momentum, we are adding CRM to our top five ideas for 2026 along with Microsoft (MSFT), Intuit (INTU), Oracle (ORCL), and Snowflake (SNOW)," said Materne and his team.

Morgan Stanley kept its Overweight rating with a $405 price target on Salesforce's stock.

"A bigger beat and slight acceleration in cRPO, coupled with an impressive ramp in Agentforce metrics, reinforce the theme of building momentum we heard at the Analyst Day. While it may take a few more data points for a skeptical investor base to come on board, at 13X EV/CY27 FCF, we are buyers here," said analysts led by Keith Weiss.

Wells Fargo maintained its Equal Weight rating on the shares with a $265 price target.

"Our estimates move higher mostly to incorporate INFA acquisition. Organic numbers largely unchanged, keeping us balanced until we gain more evidence to corroborate the anticipated Agentforce-led acceleration in 12-18 months," said analysts Michael Turrin and his team.

data.title: Nayax announces acquisition of Lynkwell
data.description:
• Nayax (NYAX) announced on Thursday the acquisition of Lynkwell, an AI-enabled EV charging platform.
  • The acquisition enables Nayax partners to access Lynkwell’s full suite of products and services designed to help deploy and operate charging infrastructure across public use cases, including retail, multi-family, workplace, fleet, and government settings. Transaction Highlights:
  • The transaction has an implied effective purchase price of $25.9MM USD in cash at closing for 100% of the business, with an additional earnout based on certain profitability metrics within the first 12 months post-closing.
  • Lynkwell’s financial performance includes audited 2024 revenues of $17.1MM USD and substantial year-over-year growth.
  • Nayax has funded the transaction with cash on hand.
  • Synergies are expected from the integration of Lynkwell into Nayax’s ecosystem, enhancing both revenue and operational efficiency.
  • Nayax (NYAX) shares were down 0.69% in premarket trading.

data.title: The 20-20 volatility club: These five consumer stocks have a high short interest position and low PE ratio
data.description:
Stocks with both a high short interest ratio and a low price-to-earnings ratio can be a value trap for investors or a contrarian opportunity with significant short squeeze potential. Stocks that have a short interest position of over 20% and a price-to-earnings ratio of below 20 can indicate that traders do not expect fundamentals to improve. However, history has shown in the consumer sector that these stocks can bolt higher if a key catalyst such as M&A intrigue, earnings upside, or a rush of social media attention appears.

 

 

 

 

Kohl's (KSS): Forward price-to-earnings ratio of 16.7, short interest of 24.8%, Wall Street analyst consensus rating of Hold, and Seeking Alpha Quant Rating of Hold.

Jack in the Box (JACK): Forward price-to-earnings ratio of 5.3, short interest of 25.8%, Wall Street analyst consensus rating of Hold, and Seeking Alpha Quant Rating of Strong Sell.

Caleres (CAL): Forward price-to-earnings ratio of 2.1, short interest of 22.2%, Wall Street analyst consensus rating of Buy, Seeking Alpha Quant Rating of Sell.

Lovesac (LOVE): Forward price-to-earnings ratio of 19.5, short interest of 26.5%, Wall Street analyst consensus rating of Strong Buy, and Seeking Alpha Quant Rating of Strong Sell.

Grocery Outlet Holding Corp. (GO): Forward price-to-earnings ratio of 14.5, short interest of 22.5%, Wall Street analyst consensus rating of Hold,  and Seeking Alpha Quant Rating of Hold.

data.title: Plug Power signs LOI with Hy2gen for electrolyzer at Sunrhyse green hydrogen project
data.description:
Plug Power (PLUG) +0.4% pre-market Thursday after saying it signed a letter of intent with Hy2gen for a 5 MW PEM electrolyzer to be installed at Sunrhyse, Hy2gen's flagship hydrogen production project in France.

Plug (PLUG) said it will support the transport and distribution of hydrogen produced at Sunrhyse and continue expanding its turnkey hydrogen forklift solutions for logistics bases across the region.

Plug (PLUG) said the LOI outlines the foundation for collaboration with Hy2gen to advance hydrogen use in southern France, and builds on the growing collaboration between the two companies across Europe and North America, including Hy2gen's Project Courant in Quebec, which includes a large-scale electrolyzer and aims to reach final investment decision before 2027.

"Expanding RFNBO-certified hydrogen production in France marks another important step in Plug’s European growth strategy," Plug (PLUG) President and Chief Revenue Officer Jose Luis Crespo said.

data.title: Trevi Therapeutics appoints new CFO
data.description:
• Trevi Therapeutics (TRVI) named David Hastings as its next chief financial officer, effective January 6, 2026.
  • Hastings prior roles include CFO positions at Arbutus, Unilife, and Incyte.
  • Hastings currently serves on the board and chairs the audit committee of Scynexis.

data.title: As Salesforce results impress, SA Quant ranks the biggest SaaS companies
data.description:

The update also comes at a time when the SaaS landscape is far from uniform.

data.title: Meren Energy renews 12-month share buyback program
data.description:
• The TSX has approved Meren Energy’s (MRNFF) normal course issuer bid ((NCIB)) share buyback program.
  • The NCIB allows Meren to repurchase up to 21.64M common shares across the TSX, Nasdaq Stockholm, and Canadian alternative trading systems.
  • The total permitted repurchase value is capped at US$35M, representing 5% of the company’s 432.7M-share public float as of November 24, 2025.
  • The buyback window runs from December 8, 2025, to the earlier of December 7, 2026, completion of the maximum repurchases, or Meren’s termination of the NCIB.

data.title: November U.S. job cuts down 53% month-over-month - Challenger Report
data.description:
U.S.-based employers announced 71.32K job cuts in November, down 53% from the 153.07K announced in October and up 24% year-over-year, Challenger, Gray, & Christmas said on Thursday.

Year-to-date through November, employers have announced 1.17M job cuts, an increase of 54% from last year. Numbers are at the highest level since 2020.

It is the sixth time since 1993 that job cuts through November have surpassed 1.1M, according to the report.

"Layoff plans fell last month, certainly a positive sign. That said, job cuts in November have risen above 70,000 only twice since 2008: in 2022 and in 2008," said Andy Challenger, workplace expert and chief revenue officer for Challenger, Gray & Christmas.

In November, restructuring led all reasons with 20.22K for a total of 128.26K in 2025. Store, unit, or department closing accounted for 17.14K in November, and 178.53K for the year.

AI was cited for 6.28K cuts in November. So far this year, AI is responsible for 54.69K layoff plans.

For hiring, employers have announced 497.15K planned hires so far this year, down 35% from the 761.95K announced last year. The year-to-date number includes 372.52K seasonal hiring plans.

"The increased spending over the Black Friday and the Thanksgiving weekend may give rise to hires in December right before the holiday. It's unclear, however, if those positions will last into the New Year," said Challenger.

The report was issued by the company ahead of the scheduled 7:30 a.m. ET release time.

data.title: Dollar General Q3 sales bolstered by more stores, increased traffic
data.description:
Shares of Dollar General (DG) are moving higher in Thursday’s premarket trading as the company’s better-than-expected third quarter results led the discount retailer to raise its full year guidance while still anticipating uncertain consumer behaviors.

For FY25, Dollar General (DG) now expects net sales to increase by 4.7% to 4.9%, up from its prior estimate of 4.3% to 4.8% growth, translating into revenue of $42.52B and $42.60B which exceeds the $42.49B consensus estimates.

Same store sales growth is now seen increasing 2.5% to 2.7% from up 2.1% to 2.6%, previously, and a profit of $6.30 to $6.50 versus prior range of $5.80 to $6.30 per share. The new EPS range exceeds the $6.14 per shares estimate.

These estimates also include the company’s plan to execute 4,885 real estate projects in FY25, including 575 new stores in the U.S. and up to 15 news stores in Mexico.

In the third quarter, with gains across all categories, Dollar General (DG) earned a profit of $1.28 per share, up 44% from a year ago and $0.35 better than expected.

Meanwhile, sales were up 4.6% to $10.65B, beating expectations by $50M on the impact of new store openings and gains within existing stores. Comparable store sales increased 2.5%, reflecting a 2.5% increase in customer traffic and unchanged average transaction amount.

Gross margin improved 107 basis points to 29.9%, driven by higher inventory markups and lower shrink, partially offset by an increased LIFO provision.

Shares last traded with a gain of more than 4%.
